behavior_summary: U8P_POLE::look runs when the player/actor 'looks' at a pole item. It marks the item with an info/process-exclude state, constructs the literal message "pole ", asks the item to 'bark' that message (Item::bark) so the message is displayed to the viewer, frees the temporary string, briefly observes the bark return value, then suspends/returns.

The routine's observable gameplay effect is to present the short description/label "pole " to the inspecting actor and ensure the item is set into a state (info + process-exclude) that prevents or alters subsequent processing.
notable_control_flow: Set item info and set process-exclude on the item (engine-level item flags) at start of handler.
Allocate/prepare a C string containing "pole ", convert to a pointer, and push it for the bark call.
Call external Item::bark from U8P_POLE::look to display the string; pass the constructed message and item/player context.
Free the constructed string after Item::bark returns.
Push Item::bark return value, use it in an 'implies' pattern and pop a temp, then suspend and return control.
open_questions: Exact numeric info id used when setting item info (disassembly shows a constant but precise semantic meaning is not documented here).
The precise calling order/roles of pushed operands for Item::bark (which pushed value corresponds to 'this' vs 'pid') is inferred from patterns but not explicitly documented.
Whether marking the item 'process-exclude' is intended as a persistent state change for gameplay (prevents future AI/processing) or merely a short-term guard in this handler.

main_function.name: U8P_POLE::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to a 'U8P_POLE::look' interaction on a pole item by setting item state and emitting the short description "pole " to the viewer via the item's bark routine.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_POLE::look) — invoked when an actor/player examines or 'looks at' the pole (inferred from name and disassembly).

function_map.name: U8P_POLE::look
function_map.role: Handle the item's U8P_POLE::look/examine event: set item flags, build and deliver the item's short description.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item API called to present a short text message from an item/process to a player/process.

external_calls.purpose: Deliver the literal text "pole " as the pole's short description to the viewing player/process.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_POLE::look runs when the player/actor 'looks' at a pole item. It marks the item with an info/process-exclude state, constructs the literal message "pole ", asks the item to 'bark' that message (Item::bark) so the message is displayed to the viewer, frees the temporary string, briefly observes the bark return value, then suspends/returns.
